title: Euclid's algorithm (TAOCP Algorithm E): division-step bound + correctness
statement_informal: Euclid's algorithm computes gcd(a,b) and performs at most b division steps. (TAOCP's first algorithm; the tight O(log) / Fibonacci bound is a separate harder unit.)

cost_model.notes: Each iteration performs one a mod b. The second argument strictly decreases (a mod b < b), so there are at most b steps.

references: Knuth, TAOCP Vol. 1 §1.1 and Vol. 2 §4.5.2 — Algorithm E (Euclidean algorithm), the first algorithm in the book.
Lamé's theorem: the worst case occurs at consecutive Fibonacci numbers (tight O(log) bound — future unit).
